title: Andhra Chicken Curry (Kodi Kura)

subs.name: 750 g bone-in mutton; increase final simmer to 35 minutes
1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder + 1 tsp cayenne
3 tbsp cashew paste
subs.notes: adds richness without the coconut flavour

steps.text: Marinate the chicken with turmeric, half the chilli powder and a pinch of salt for 15 minutes while you prepare the rest.
Dry-roast the grated coconut in a small pan over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until it turns a nutty golden brown, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate and let it cool.

Heat oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Fry two-thirds of the sliced onion, stirring often, until it turns deep brown and crisp, about 12 minutes. Remove and cool slightly, then grind with the toasted coconut and a splash of water to a smooth paste.
In the same oil, splutter mustard seeds and add curry leaves. Add the remaining onion and sauté until soft. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry until the raw smell disappears, about 2 minutes.

Add tomatoes with a pinch of salt, cook until they soften and the oil starts to separate, about 5 minutes. Add the remaining chilli powder, coriander powder and garam masala; stir for 30 seconds on low heat so the spices don't burn.
Increase the flame, add the marinated chicken and sear, turning the pieces, until they are no longer pink on the outside, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the onion-coconut paste and 300 ml hot water. Mix well, cover, and cook on medium flame for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the gravy darkens.
Remove the lid, cook on high for 3–4 minutes to reduce the gravy to a thick coating consistency. Check salt, scatter coriander leaves and rest 5 minutes before serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: coconut → 3 tbsp cashew paste (adds richness without the coconut flavour); chicken → 750 g bone-in mutton; increase final simmer to 35 minutes; red-chilli-powder → 1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder + 1 tsp cayenne (balances colour with moderate heat)
